assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 18-23: 'TC-8001 has stream suffix `[rhtpa-2.2]`, which maps to stream **2.2.x**. TC-8006 has stream suffix `[rhtpa-2.1]`, which maps to stream **2.1.x**. These are **different streams**. TC-8001 is NOT a same-stream duplicate.' Line 27 further confirms: 'TC-8001 is a **different-stream sibling** (companion tracker).'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 29-38: 'Before creating a "Related" link to TC-8001, the skill checks the current issue's `issuelinks` array (already fetched in Step 1). Existing links on TC-8006: - Link ID 1990401: type.name = "Related", outwardIssue.key = "TC-8001"'. The check explicitly examines the issuelinks array for type.name and outwardIssue.key criteria before deciding whether to create a link.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 40-46: '**All criteria satisfied.** A matching Related link to TC-8001 already exists. **Action: Skip link creation.** Log: "Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping" No `jira.create_link()` call is made. The existing link (ID 1990401) is preserved as-is.' Also confirmed in triage-outcome.md line 30: 'No `jira.create_link()` call was made.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 44: 'Log: "Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping"'. Also triage-outcome.md line 28: 'The skill logged: **"Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping"**'

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 57-64 presents the full sibling landscape table after the link skip decision: '| Issue | Stream | Status | Affects Versions | |------------|--------|-------------|-------------------------------| | TC-8001 | 2.2.x | In Progress | RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1 | | TC-8006 <--| 2.1.x | New | RHTPA 2.1.0 |'. The table appears under the '### Sibling Landscape' heading, showing that the summary is presented regardless of the link creation being skipped.
